methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Periodic Labs is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Liam Fedus and Ekin Dogus Cubuk that has raised $300M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables (Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, Together AI), supplemented by reference to a broader set of 8 private peers and 1 public comp (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $300M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying $750M. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables yields a median valuation of $15.0B, with a range of $8.3B to $25.0B. The peer-median method reflects valuations of Series A–stage AI companies in the target's competitive space.

The triangulated valuation range is $638M (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $7.9B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-specific valuation and limited visibility into company-specific traction metrics. Key uncertainties include the applicability of Series A peer valuations to a Seed-stage company and the adequacy of total raised as a valuation proxy absent revenue or growth-rate data. The median estimate is conservative relative to the peer universe but recognizes execution risk at the Seed stage.
